examples: Today it's pretty desolate, barren and extremely hot.
He lives in a great, big, desolate old house in the country and no one goes near him.
When plants first made the transition ashore more than 400 million years ago, the land was barren and desolate, inhospitable to life.
He's also the author of Desolate Angel: Jack Kerouac, the Beat Generation, and America.
The great red hills stand desolate and the earth has torn away like flesh.
